_schema_notes.coverage: Authoritative schema for all DID formats — DD Form 1664 (pre-2014) and free-text (post-2014 MIL-STD-963C)
_schema_notes.did_format: Set to 'dd_form_1664' or 'free_text'. Determines which fields are expected to be populated.
_schema_notes.963c_compliant: True if the DID was prepared under MIL-STD-963C (2014 or later). False for older DIDs.
_schema_notes.reserved_fields: Fields marked RESERVED are present in one format but not the other. Always include them — set to empty string or empty array when not applicable.
_schema_notes.preparation_instructions: Official MIL-STD-963C name for what was previously called 'requirements'. Contains the numbered sections of the DID. All section numbers are normalized to strictly numeric dot-notation (1, 1.1, 1.1.1). Letter-based source numbering (2.a, 3.1.b) is converted to numeric equivalents (2.1, 3.1.2).
_schema_notes.asterisk: True if the requirement number is followed by * in the source document (conditional requirement)

title: Quality Assessment Report
summary: This Data Item Description provides format and content for a Quality Assessment Report, detailing inspection, test data, and quality issues for complex systems and components.
description_purpose: The Quality Assessment Report provides inspection (examination and test) data. The report is used to assess process and product conformance to technical requirements for quality, reliability, and functional performance, identify problems, and initiate corrective actions or quality improvements.
application_interrelationship: This Data Item Description (DID) contains the format and content preparation instructions for the data product generated by the specific and discrete task requirement as delineated in the contract. This DID is applicable for complex systems, major items, subsystems, equipment, and components. This DID is essential for complex systems and major items.
distribution_statement: DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT A: Approved for public release; distribution is unlimited.

children.title: Format.
children.content: The Quality Assessment Report shall be contractor selected. Unless effective presentation would be degraded, the initially used format arrangement shall be used for all subsequent submissions.

children.title: Content.
children.content: The Quality Assessment Report shall contain tables, charts, photographs, and narrative analysis of quality status, problems, and corrective action taken, or recommended for the following areas:

children.title: Receiving inspection.
children.content: Provide statistical evidence that incoming supplies conform to established quality requirements, to include results of vendor rating and vendor surveillance activities.

children.title: Manufacturing operations.
children.content: Provide evidence that statistical in-process control of quality is maintained in all areas of manufacturing processes.

children.title: Special processes.
children.content: Provide failure rates (the ratio of first test failures divided by the first test failures plus the first test successes) for major system assemblies, subsystems, and systems functional test at the various functional checkpoints during manufacturing and testing, supported with tables to associate the failure with the next lower assembly/subassembly of functional circuitry, as appropriate. Failure rates for reworked or rebuilt assemblies shall be segregated and identified.

children.title: Assembly operation.
children.content: Provide documentation to reflect the frequency and type of problems associated with major items of system equipment.

children.title: Final inspection.
children.content: Provide documentation of the analysis of quality data to show that process and product quality problem areas have been identified and corrective action implemented.

children.title: Field performance.
children.content: Provide documentation assessing the quality status of field performance.

children.title: Special problems.
children.content: Provide items of special interest, i.e., continuing quality or design problems, based on information generated during quality audit, quality surveys, or investigation of field complaints.

children.title: Inspection equipment.
children.content: Provide detailed qualitative and quantitative results, including test conditions, methods and inspection equipment used in the examination of the system or item.
